pi_items.verified_value: 40 observations of household weekly food expenditure (y) and weekly income in $100 increments (x); real household data from textbook dataset
pi_items.source: Analysis of official food.csv from POE5 repository; Breusch-Pagan test p-value=0.0066 and White's test p-value=0.0229 both show significant heteroscedasticity

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is a real-world dataset of 40 household observations from Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition. When regressing food expenditure on income, the income coefficient is 10.209643 (exactly matching the claimed 10.2096) and R-squared is 0.385002 (exactly matching the claimed 0.385). Both Breusch-Pagan test (p=0.0066) and White's test (p=0.0229) indicate significant heteroscedasticity at the 0.05 significance level, justifying the use of robust standard errors in the task. The data source is the official POE5 repository at http://www.principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/data/csv/food.csv.
